mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP growth and steady employment expansion increase corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in >50% of instances where GDPC1 and PAYEMS show concurrent month-over-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid federal funds rate hikes compress equity valuations by increasing the discount rate applied to future cash flows, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive or neutral in >60% of triggered instances despite rate hikes exceeding the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in the unemployment rate and increases in nonfarm payrolls confirm labor market tightening, reducing wage-inflation fears and boosting equity risk appetite for positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in >55% of observations, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production exceeding 108 indicates robust manufacturing output and capacity utilization, signaling strong aggregate supply conditions that support corporate margins and drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in >50% of periods where INDPRO remains above 108, indicating supply strength no longer translates to equity gains.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect improving consumer confidence and credit availability, leading to immediate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors price in near-term economic resilience.
failure_condition: Housing starts increases consistently precede negative 5-day SPX returns in >60% of cases over a 3-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in retail sales volume signals weakening consumer demand, which directly reduces corporate revenue forecasts and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally or remain flat in >65% of instances following a retail sales decline exceeding the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising consumer prices and an unchanged federal funds rate creates a negative real interest rate environment that erodes purchasing power and forces equity repricing, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint CPI and FEDFUNDS signal yields positive forward returns in >50% of observations, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate stays below 4.0%, the labor market operates at peak efficiency, minimizing recession risks and sustaining high equity valuations for positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative in >55% of periods where UNRATE remains under 4.0%, indicating the low-unemployment premium has been fully priced in or reversed.
